---
title: "A spread of simulations"
subtitle: "One engine, many living systems — hover a title to read its spec"
resources:
  - gallery/
---

The same engine and registry produce qualitatively different living systems by
changing only the declarative spec. Each clip below is one `spec.yaml` over the
identical operators and schedule grammar; **hover (or tab to) a simulation's title
to read the exact spec that generated it.** The first two are validated to
floating-point precision against [ParticleGraph](https://github.com/allierc/ParticleGraph).

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## How a clip is made
`Plexus_Main.py -o generate_plot <config>` runs the forward simulation, writes the
trajectory, and renders the movie — no per-simulation code, only the spec differs.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
.sim-gallery{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(220px,1fr));gap:1.1rem;margin:1rem 0 2rem}
.sim-card{margin:0}
.sim-card video{width:100%;border-radius:7px;background:#000;display:block;aspect-ratio:1/1;object-fit:cover}
.sim-card figcaption{margin-top:.35rem;line-height:1.25}
.sim-name{font-weight:600;cursor:help;position:relative;border-bottom:1px dotted #999}
.sim-cap{display:block;font-size:.84em;color:#777;margin-top:.1rem}
.sim-spec{display:none;position:absolute;left:0;top:1.5em;z-index:40;width:max(300px,108%);max-height:360px;overflow:auto;
  background:#1e1e1e;border-radius:7px;box-shadow:0 8px 28px rgba(0,0,0,.4);padding:.5rem .7rem;text-align:left;cursor:auto}
.sim-name:hover .sim-spec,.sim-name:focus .sim-spec{display:block}
.sim-spec pre{margin:0;font-size:.72em;line-height:1.3;white-space:pre;color:#e9e9e9;background:none;border:none;padding:0}
</style>

<h2>Attraction–repulsion &amp; boids — validated against ParticleGraph</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/arbitrary_3.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">arbitrary_3<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Full match to ParticleGraph arbitrary_3_1: 3 types, 30000 particles, sigma=0.005,
# max_radius=0.075, periodic, first_derivative, dt=0.1, 250 frames. Now feasible
# because attraction_repulsion runs on a radius neighbour graph (O(E)), not dense.
general:
  name: arbitrary_3
  seed: 0
  n_frames: 250
  dt: 0.1
  boundary: periodic

sets:
  particle:
    n: 30000
    types:
      t0: {fraction: 0.34, p: [1.6233, 1.0413, 1.6012, 1.5615]}
      t1: {fraction: 0.33, p: [1.7667, 1.8308, 1.0855, 1.9055]}
      t2: {fraction: 0.33, p: [1.7226, 1.7850, 1.0584, 1.8579]}

fields: {}

operators:
  - {op: radius_graph,         at: particle, radius: 0.075}
  - {op: attraction_repulsion, at: particle, sigma: 0.005}

schedule: [radius_graph, attraction_repulsion]

plotting:                      # render STYLE (read by plexus.plot; engine ignores it)
  background: black
  colormap: tab10
  point_size: 2.0</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">3 types, 30k particles; per-type difference-of-Gaussians law (reproduces PDE_A)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/boids_16.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">boids_16<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Exact reproduction of ParticleGraph boids reference graphs_data/boids/boids:
# 1792 particles, 16 types (112 each), 4000 frames, min_radius=0.001,
# max_radius=0.04, dt=0.5, periodic, 2nd_derivative, mean aggregation.
# Per-type p = [cohesion, alignment, separation].
general:
  name: boids_16
  seed: 0
  n_frames: 4000
  dt: 0.5
  boundary: periodic

sets:
  particle:
    n: 1792
    vel_init: 0.0004
    types:
      t00: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [27.600000381469727, 92.5, 48.20000076293945]}
      t01: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [32.0, 51.79999923706055, 29.799999237060547]}
      t02: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [23.600000381469727, 35.0, 13.5]}
      t03: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [3.299999952316284, 76.4000015258789, 13.0]}
      t04: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [94.0, 78.69999694824219, 30.799999237060547]}
      t05: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [81.4000015258789, 34.599998474121094, 2.200000047683716]}
      t06: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [3.0999999046325684, 40.5, 17.0]}
      t07: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [88.80000305175781, 7.199999809265137, 29.799999237060547]}
      t08: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [32.70000076293945, 76.80000305175781, 26.100000381469727]}
      t09: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [14.5, 56.79999923706055, 27.600000381469727]}
      t10: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [63.29999923706055, 99.9000015258789, 13.899999618530273]}
      t11: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [32.5, 25.200000762939453, 24.399999618530273]}
      t12: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [97.5999984741211, 56.5, 12.100000381469727]}
      t13: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [62.20000076293945, 9.100000381469727, 28.299999237060547]}
      t14: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [76.4000015258789, 52.900001525878906, 32.0]}
      t15: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [48.099998474121094, 54.400001525878906, 30.700000762939453]}

fields: {}

operators:
  - {op: radius_graph, at: particle, radius: 0.04, min_radius: 0.001}
  - {op: boids,        at: particle}

schedule: [radius_graph, boids]

plotting:
  background: black
  colormap: tab20
  point_size: 6.0</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">16 types of boid; cohesion/alignment/separation (reproduces PDE_B)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<h2>Ant colony — foraging &amp; stigmergy</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ant_colony.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ant_colony<span class="sim-spec"><pre># ant_colony -- ant colony (Lague Ant-Simulation) over the Plexus registry; only spec params differ.
name: ant_colony
seed: 0
boundary: wall
n_frames: 1800
record_every: 8
sets:
  cell:
    n: 140
    start: [0.40, 0.40, 0.60, 0.60]
    types: {ant: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 120}}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 20, radius: 0.010, density: 1.0}
fields:
  food_pher: {frame: grid, res: 140, diffusion: 0.02, decay: 0.02, couples_to: cell}
  home_pher: {frame: grid, res: 140, diffusion: 0.02, decay: 0.02, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: motility, at: cell, speed: 260.0, rot: 0.12}
  - {op: colony, at: cell, home: [0.5, 0.5, 0.055], food: [[0.86, 0.86, 0.07], [0.14, 0.86, 0.07], [0.86, 0.14, 0.07]], perception: 0.09, turn_noise: 0.5, food_stock: 0.0}
  - {op: trail,   at: &quot;cell[loaded=0]&quot;, from: food_pher, turn: 0.45, sensor_dist: 0.05, sensor_angle: 0.6}
  - {op: trail,   at: &quot;cell[loaded=1]&quot;, from: home_pher, turn: 0.45, sensor_dist: 0.05, sensor_angle: 0.6}
  - {op: secrete, at: &quot;cell[loaded=0]&quot;, to: home_pher, rate: 4.0, runout: 400}
  - {op: secrete, at: &quot;cell[loaded=1]&quot;, to: food_pher, rate: 4.0, runout: 400}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 8, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 1200, drag: 0.6, vmax: 6.0}
schedule: [aggregate, trail, colony, motility, secrete, food_pher.diffuse, home_pher.diffuse, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">a colony forages and lays trails</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ant_highway.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ant_highway<span class="sim-spec"><pre># ant_highway -- ant colony (Lague Ant-Simulation) over the Plexus registry; only spec params differ.
name: ant_highway
seed: 0
boundary: wall
n_frames: 2400
record_every: 8
sets:
  cell:
    n: 180
    start: [0.05,0.40,0.24,0.60]
    types: {ant: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 120}}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 20, radius: 0.010, density: 1.0}
fields:
  food_pher: {frame: grid, res: 140, diffusion: 0.02, decay: 0.02, couples_to: cell}
  home_pher: {frame: grid, res: 140, diffusion: 0.02, decay: 0.02, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: motility, at: cell, speed: 260.0, rot: 0.12}
  - {op: colony, at: cell, home: [0.14, 0.5, 0.05], food: [[0.82, 0.5, 0.1]], perception: 0.09, turn_noise: 0.5, food_stock: 0.0}
  - {op: trail,   at: &quot;cell[loaded=0]&quot;, from: food_pher, turn: 0.45, sensor_dist: 0.05, sensor_angle: 0.6}
  - {op: trail,   at: &quot;cell[loaded=1]&quot;, from: home_pher, turn: 0.45, sensor_dist: 0.05, sensor_angle: 0.6}
  - {op: secrete, at: &quot;cell[loaded=0]&quot;, to: home_pher, rate: 4.0, runout: 400}
  - {op: secrete, at: &quot;cell[loaded=1]&quot;, to: food_pher, rate: 4.0, runout: 400}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 8, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 1200, drag: 0.6, vmax: 6.0}
schedule: [aggregate, trail, colony, motility, secrete, food_pher.diffuse, home_pher.diffuse, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">trails coarsen into a dominant highway</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ant_islands.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ant_islands<span class="sim-spec"><pre># ant_islands -- ant colony (Lague Ant-Simulation) over the Plexus registry; only spec params differ.
name: ant_islands
seed: 1
boundary: wall
n_frames: 2600
record_every: 8
obstacles:
  - [0.5, 0.78, 0.07]
  - [0.78, 0.5, 0.07]
  - [0.22, 0.5, 0.07]
  - [0.5, 0.22, 0.07]
sets:
  cell:
    n: 200
    start: [0.42,0.42,0.58,0.58]
    types: {ant: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 120}}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 20, radius: 0.010, density: 1.0}
fields:
  food_pher: {frame: grid, res: 140, diffusion: 0.02, decay: 0.02, couples_to: cell}
  home_pher: {frame: grid, res: 140, diffusion: 0.02, decay: 0.02, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: motility, at: cell, speed: 260.0, rot: 0.12}
  - {op: colony, at: cell, home: [0.5, 0.5, 0.055], food: [[0.85, 0.85, 0.05], [0.15, 0.85, 0.05], [0.85, 0.15, 0.05], [0.15, 0.15, 0.05]], perception: 0.09, turn_noise: 0.5, food_stock: 0.0}
  - {op: trail,   at: &quot;cell[loaded=0]&quot;, from: food_pher, turn: 0.45, sensor_dist: 0.05, sensor_angle: 0.6}
  - {op: trail,   at: &quot;cell[loaded=1]&quot;, from: home_pher, turn: 0.45, sensor_dist: 0.05, sensor_angle: 0.6}
  - {op: secrete, at: &quot;cell[loaded=0]&quot;, to: home_pher, rate: 4.0, runout: 400}
  - {op: secrete, at: &quot;cell[loaded=1]&quot;, to: food_pher, rate: 4.0, runout: 400}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 8, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 1200, drag: 0.6, vmax: 6.0}
schedule: [aggregate, trail, colony, motility, secrete, food_pher.diffuse, home_pher.diffuse, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">food on separate islands</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ant_many_food.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ant_many_food<span class="sim-spec"><pre># ant_many_food -- ant colony (Lague Ant-Simulation) over the Plexus registry; only spec params differ.
name: ant_many_food
seed: 0
boundary: wall
n_frames: 1800
record_every: 8
sets:
  cell:
    n: 140
    start: [0.40, 0.40, 0.60, 0.60]
    types: {ant: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 120}}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 20, radius: 0.010, density: 1.0}
fields:
  food_pher: {frame: grid, res: 140, diffusion: 0.02, decay: 0.02, couples_to: cell}
  home_pher: {frame: grid, res: 140, diffusion: 0.02, decay: 0.02, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: motility, at: cell, speed: 260.0, rot: 0.12}
  - {op: colony, at: cell, home: [0.5, 0.5, 0.055], food: [[0.85, 0.85, 0.06], [0.15, 0.85, 0.06], [0.85, 0.15, 0.06], [0.15, 0.15, 0.06], [0.5, 0.88, 0.06]], perception: 0.09, turn_noise: 0.5, food_stock: 0.0}
  - {op: trail,   at: &quot;cell[loaded=0]&quot;, from: food_pher, turn: 0.45, sensor_dist: 0.05, sensor_angle: 0.6}
  - {op: trail,   at: &quot;cell[loaded=1]&quot;, from: home_pher, turn: 0.45, sensor_dist: 0.05, sensor_angle: 0.6}
  - {op: secrete, at: &quot;cell[loaded=0]&quot;, to: home_pher, rate: 4.0, runout: 400}
  - {op: secrete, at: &quot;cell[loaded=1]&quot;, to: food_pher, rate: 4.0, runout: 400}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 8, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 1200, drag: 0.6, vmax: 6.0}
schedule: [aggregate, trail, colony, motility, secrete, food_pher.diffuse, home_pher.diffuse, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">many food sources, competing trails</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<h2>Slime mould — Physarum networks</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_default.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_default<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_default -- one species, the canonical Physarum transport network.
# Port of SebLague/Slime-Simulation default settings: agents spawned in a disc at
# centre, sense 3 sensors, steer toward the strongest trail, deposit + diffuse +
# decay -&gt; a self-reinforcing network that grows and coarsens (stigmergy).
general: {name: slime_default, seed: 0, n_frames: 400, record_every: 4, dt: 1.0, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 12000
    spawn: disc
    spawn_radius: 0.3
    types:
      a: {fraction: 1.0, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.45, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.025, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 220, deposit: 0.9, decay: 0.012, diffuse: 0.35, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.2, 0.95, 0.65]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">self-reinforcing transport network</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_curly.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_curly<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_curly -- wide sensor cone + high turn -&gt; curly, restless, foam-like texture.
general: {name: slime_curly, seed: 0, n_frames: 400, record_every: 4, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 13000
    spawn: disc
    spawn_radius: 0.3
    types:
      a: {fraction: 1.0, move_speed: 0.005, turn_speed: 0.9, sensor_angle: 45, sensor_dist: 0.02, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 220, deposit: 0.9, decay: 0.014, diffuse: 0.35, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.2, 0.95, 0.65]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">high turning rate, curly filaments</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_filaments.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_filaments<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_filaments -- narrow sensor cone + low turn -&gt; long, straight, branching filaments.
general: {name: slime_filaments, seed: 0, n_frames: 450, record_every: 4, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 13000
    spawn: disc
    spawn_radius: 0.3
    types:
      a: {fraction: 1.0, move_speed: 0.007, turn_speed: 0.18, sensor_angle: 14, sensor_dist: 0.035, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 220, deposit: 0.9, decay: 0.01, diffuse: 0.3, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.2, 0.95, 0.65]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">fine filamentary structure</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_eight.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_eight<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_eight -- EIGHT mutually-repelling species (cross=-1): eight colour territories.
# The framework is channel-agnostic -- one type = one trail channel = one colour, so
# going 4 -&gt; 8 species is purely more `types` + more `plotting.colors`, no code change.
# Lowest deposit / highest decay / most agents of the suite: eight overlapping channels
# saturate (and additive-blend to white) fastest, so the density is the most restrained.
general: {name: slime_eight, seed: 0, n_frames: 440, record_every: 4, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 24000
    spawn: random
    types:
      a: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      b: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      c: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      d: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      e: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      f: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      g: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      h: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 256, deposit: 0.33, decay: 0.05, diffuse: 0.24, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.20, 0.95, 0.65]
    b: [1.00, 0.35, 0.45]
    c: [0.40, 0.65, 1.00]
    d: [1.00, 0.85, 0.30]
    e: [0.95, 0.40, 0.95]
    f: [1.00, 0.60, 0.20]
    g: [0.20, 0.85, 0.85]
    h: [0.65, 0.50, 1.00]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">eight sources</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<h2>MPM fluids &amp; materials</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mat_liquid.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mat_liquid<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: mat_liquid
seed: 0
n_frames: 500
record_every: 2
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 1
    start: [0.5, 0.80, 0.5, 0.80]
    types:
      ball: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 200, layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 80, material: liquid}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 2200, radius: 0.11, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 12, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.1, wall_damp: 0.9, surface_tension: 4000.0}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">liquid material (no shear memory)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mat_elastic.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mat_elastic<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: mat_elastic
seed: 0
n_frames: 500
record_every: 2
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 1
    start: [0.5, 0.80, 0.5, 0.80]
    types:
      ball: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 200, layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 200, material: elastic}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 2200, radius: 0.11, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 12, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.1, wall_damp: 0.9}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">elastic material (shape memory)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ph_crown_splash.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ph_crown_splash<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: ph_crown_splash
seed: 0
n_frames: 900
record_every: 5
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 2
    start: [0.5,0.86,0.5,0.86]
    types:
      water: {fraction: 0.5, youngs: 300, block: [0.03,0.03,0.97,0.38], layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 300, material: liquid}]}
      ball: {fraction: 0.5, youngs: 500, layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 500, material: elastic}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 7000, radius: 0.10, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 18, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.1, wall_damp: 0.5, wall_contact: 0.06, surface_tension: 10}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">a drop hits a pool: crown splash</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ph_slosh.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ph_slosh<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: ph_slosh
seed: 0
n_frames: 1000
record_every: 5
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 1
    start: [0.5,0.2,0.5,0.2]
    types:
      water: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 300, block: [0.05,0.04,0.95,0.30], layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 300, material: liquid}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 9000, radius: 0.30, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0, gx: 6.0, gy: -10.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 18, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.15, wall_damp: 0.5, wall_contact: 0.06, surface_tension: 10}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">sloshing in a vessel</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<h2>Microswimmers</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/motile.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">motile<span class="sim-spec"><pre># A motile (swimming) ciliate: the sphere self-propels at U = (2/3) B_1 along its
# axis, sweeping through the nutrient bath and leaving a depleted wake (lab frame).
# Same registry as sessile.yaml -- only the type (lifestyle) + the swim op differ.
name: motile
seed: 0
n_frames: 200
record_every: 2
dt: 1.0
world: 1.6
sets:
  organism:
    n: 1
    radius: 0.12
    n_mode: 40
    axis: 0.0
    types:
      motile: {fraction: 1.0, lifestyle: motile, feeding_area: 0.5}
  surface_node: {parent: organism, per_parent: 240, role: membrane}
fields:
  flow:     {kind: flow, res: 192, couples_to: organism}
  chemical: {kind: chemical, res: 192, couples_to: surface_node, diffusion: 0.05, peclet: 120.0, c_inf: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: squirmer_flow, at: organism, to: flow, frame: lab}
  - {op: slip,   at: surface_node}
  - {op: swim,   at: &quot;organism[type=motile]&quot;, gain: 0.012}
  - {op: absorb, at: &quot;surface_node[type=mouth]&quot;, from: chemical, rate: 1.0}
schedule: [squirmer_flow, slip, swim, absorb, chemical.diffuse]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">active self-propelled swimmers</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/feeding.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">feeding<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Phagotrophy: a sessile ciliate sits in a gentle ambient current (drift) that
# carries food parcels (tracers) past it left-&gt;right. Its feeding current bends the
# nearby parcels and its absorbing mouth -- here facing UPSTREAM -- EATS the ones
# that arrive. Parcels leaving the domain respawn at the left inlet, so the stream
# is continuous and the eaten count climbs.
#
# Adds one set (tracer) + two operators (advect_particles, capture) to sessile.yaml
# -- same registry, no engine change. Reference: Liu, Costello &amp; Kanso, Nat Commun
# 16, 4154 (2025).
name: feeding
seed: 0
n_frames: 360
record_every: 2
dt: 1.0
world: 1.6
sets:
  organism:
    n: 1
    radius: 0.14
    n_mode: 40
    axis: 0.0
    types:
      sessile: {fraction: 1.0, lifestyle: sessile, feeding_area: 0.3, mouth_offset: 3.14159}
  surface_node: {parent: organism, per_parent: 240, role: membrane}
  tracer: {n: 900}
fields:
  flow:     {kind: flow, res: 176, couples_to: organism}
  chemical: {kind: chemical, res: 176, couples_to: surface_node, diffusion: 0.06, peclet: 120.0, c_inf: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: squirmer_flow,    at: organism, to: flow, frame: body}
  - {op: slip,             at: surface_node}
  - {op: absorb,           at: &quot;surface_node[type=mouth]&quot;, from: chemical, rate: 1.0}
  - {op: advect_particles, at: tracer, from: flow, speed: 0.04, drift: [0.006, 0.0]}
  - {op: capture,          at: tracer, margin: 0.25}
schedule: [squirmer_flow, slip, absorb, chemical.diffuse, advect_particles, capture]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">feeding on a resource field</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/sessile.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">sessile<span class="sim-spec"><pre># A sessile (attached) ciliate: anchored sphere whose cilia drive a feeding current
# over a 50%-coverage mouth cap. Reproduces the sessile column of the paper&#x27;s
# flow + concentration figures (Liu, Costello &amp; Kanso, Nat Commun 2025).
name: sessile
seed: 0
n_frames: 120
record_every: 2
dt: 1.0
world: 1.0
sets:
  organism:
    n: 1
    radius: 0.14
    n_mode: 40
    types:
      sessile: {fraction: 1.0, lifestyle: sessile, feeding_area: 0.2}
  surface_node: {parent: organism, per_parent: 240, role: membrane}
fields:
  flow:     {kind: flow, res: 160, couples_to: organism}
  chemical: {kind: chemical, res: 160, couples_to: surface_node, diffusion: 0.06, peclet: 100.0, c_inf: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: squirmer_flow, at: organism, to: flow, frame: body}
  - {op: slip,    at: surface_node}
  - {op: absorb,  at: &quot;surface_node[type=mouth]&quot;, from: chemical, rate: 1.0}
schedule: [squirmer_flow, slip, absorb, chemical.diffuse]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">attached, beating cilia</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<h2>Dictyostelium — aggregation</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/dicty_aggregate.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">dicty_aggregate<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Dictyostelium aggregation: scattered amoebae secrete cAMP, climb its self-generated
# gradient (chemotaxis), and coalesce into streams + compact mounds. `spring` gives
# volume exclusion (repulsion) so mounds have finite size; `divide` is the
# proliferation lever (0 here -- aggregation barely divides; the optimizer sweeps it).
name: dicty_aggregate
seed: 0
boundary: periodic
n_frames: 300
record_every: 6
dt: 0.5
vmax: 0.06                # per-frame speed cap (sub-cell); keeps chemotaxis stable
sets:
  cell:
    n: 767                # real frame-0 cell count (make_target.py detected it)
    buffer: 2400          # dormant headroom for `divide`
    init_npz: target_density.npz   # seed frame 0 from the REAL cell positions
    types: {dicty: {fraction: 1.0}}
fields:
  camp: {res: 160, diffusion: 0.008, decay: 0.10, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  # spring repulsion + sigmoid-gated adhesion (user&#x27;s cell_gnn dicty law); kadh=0 -&gt;
  # pure volume exclusion (adhesion freezes a lattice and fights aggregation).
  - {op: spring,      at: cell, k_rep: 30.0, r0: 0.02, kadh: 0.0, r_on: 0.02, delta: 0.001, mu_f: 0.05}
  - {op: secrete,     at: cell, to: camp,   rate: 4.0}     # lay cAMP
  - {op: sense,       at: cell, from: camp, gain: 10.0}    # chemotaxis up self-generated gradient
  - {op: random_walk, at: cell, strength: 0.005}           # symmetry-breaking jitter
  - {op: divide,      at: cell, rate: 0.0}                 # proliferation rate (swept by optimizer)
schedule: [spring, secrete, camp.diffuse, sense, random_walk, integrate, divide]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">chemotactic aggregation to a mound</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/dicty_winner.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">dicty_winner<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: dicty_opt_winner_1
seed: 0
boundary: periodic
n_frames: 300
record_every: 6
dt: 0.6895121324729194
vmax: 0.06
sets:
  cell:
    n: 718
    buffer: 2400
    init_npz: target_density.npz
    types:
      dicty:
        fraction: 1.0
fields:
  camp:
    res: 160
    diffusion: 0.016452134544805177
    decay: 0.27644400586498485
    couples_to: cell
operators:
- op: spring
  at: cell
  k_rep: 52.46450430370259
  r0: 0.03188489682951995
  kadh: 0.0
  r_on: 0.03188489682951995
  delta: 0.001
  mu_f: 0.06348999931723383
- op: secrete
  at: cell
  to: camp
  rate: 1.1487487197567618
- op: sense
  at: cell
  from: camp
  gain: 3.5569939095753984
- op: random_walk
  at: cell
  strength: 0.028052172713633045
- op: divide
  at: cell
  rate: 0.8093601412916109
schedule:
- spring
- secrete
- camp.diffuse
- sense
- random_walk
- integrate
- divide</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">an optimized aggregation design</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>
```
